[2018-12-31 17:25] subido (Subido (rovaniemi)): Assuming that I have enough pixies, rhinos or other sources of modular damage. When should I focus on destroying 2 engineers vs focusing damage on walls? I'd think that if the opponent is more econ focused than me,  I should destroy the walls so they run out faster. On the other hand, focusing on walls lets the opponent generate more damage because walls are more efficient cost-wise.
[2018-12-31 17:28] jamberine: pixies should always be hitting 1 health defenders
[2018-12-31 17:28] jamberine: or stuff that is worth more than just soak like rhinos
[2018-12-31 17:29] satraskate (Satra): What's a one health defender? like engineer?
[2018-12-31 17:29] jamberine: I wanted to include husk and stuff
[2018-12-31 17:29] jamberine: but it's pretty much just engineer
[2018-12-31 17:30] jamberine: always is a little too strong
[2018-12-31 17:30] jamberine: if you can make your opponent nongranular next turn and you can take advantage it's definitely ok to have pixies hit walls to do so
[2018-12-31 17:32] jamberine: you should pretty much always click a pixie if you know it'll hit an engineer though, unless you need to aim for damage against frontline or something
[2018-12-31 17:32] jamberine: the idea is that if you don't, they can reserve the eneineer for when you click the pixie
[2018-12-31 17:33] jamberine: or use it as granularity against something else
[2018-12-31 17:33] jamberine: making them better off
[2018-12-31 17:39] satraskate (Satra): so almost always click engi?
[2018-12-31 17:45] subido (Subido (rovaniemi)): If you have 1 tarsier and 3 pixies when the opponent has a wall and 2 engineers, you probably shouldn't click. There are exceptions if the opponent isn't getting more granularity for next turn and you are ramping up your attack, but generally the question is this: Does clicking this pixie destroy a 1 health unit? If yes, click it. Otherwise don't
[2018-12-31 17:48] subido (Subido (rovaniemi)): Or: does clicking a pixie reduce the amount of absorb the opponent gets? Click if yes
[2018-12-31 17:51] satraskate (Satra): what about nitrocybes they usually more for defence right?
[2018-12-31 17:57] jamberine: going back to the original question, with rhinos you also have to take into account that you get to soak it after
[2018-12-31 17:57] jamberine: so you have to weigh the soak next turn versus the possibility of exploiting
[2018-12-31 17:57] jamberine: also, if it's 2 stamina you're delaying the 2nd charge as well
[2018-12-31 17:58] jamberine: I don't think the econ of the opponent matters
[2018-12-31 17:58] jamberine: you still get all your damage in the end
[2018-12-31 17:58] jamberine: it's just a matter of when
[2018-12-31 17:59] satraskate (Satra): congrats on making top 5 i saw too

[2019-01-11 10:49] drevoed: <https://www.twitch.tv/videos/359893275?t=7h28m46s>
@xYotsuba @coffeeyay 
`
xyotsuba1:can u explain why splitter hold is better than rhino here?
`
I just wanted to see what math has to say about it.
Assuming you can spend all your resourses eficiently either way, your and enemy's granularity are non-issuees, yada yada, we are in a vacuum.
If you only need 2 defence, zero threat soak rhino vs hold splitter is roughly -5R vs -(attacker cost from 5R to 6B) -X \* (half a turn inflation^2 - half a turn inflation), so soaking rhino is much better in a vacuum. Even if we count always attacking splitter as fast tarsier value, 5R, it's still a bad deal by roughly half a gold, because we use the extra 1 defence later than the opponent.
If you need 3 defence, it's -5R vs -(average attacker cost from 5R to 6B) **+**X \* (half a turn inflation - 1), so answer depends on the average attacker cost and the barrier value.
